Eastman Gang.description: The Eastman Gang was a predominantly Jewish-American street gang that dominated parts of New York City's underworld from the late 1890s to the early 1910s. Founded by Monk Eastman, the gang operated in Manhattan's Lower East Side, engaging in armed robbery, theft, illegal gambling, extortion, prostitution, and opium peddling. It succeeded the Whyos as a major non-Irish gang and had strong political connections through Tammany Hall. The gang dissolved in the early 1910s after internal conflicts and the death of its leader.

descriptions: Batavia Street Gang was affiliated with Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliated with the Eastman Gang during the turn of the 20th century

descriptions: The Crazy Butch Gang was allied to the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s as they were incorporated into the Squab Wheelmen , an ally of Monk Eastman's organization.

descriptions: Batavia Street Gang is listed as an ally of Eastman Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang , Lenox Avenue Gang

descriptions: The Eastman Gang was at war with the Five Points Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s

descriptions: Eastman Gang and Five Points Gang both succeeded the Whyos, and later warred over territory and political work.
edges.evidence_quote: Along with the increasingly Italian-American and Italian immigrant Five Points Gang under Italian-American Paolo Antonio Vaccarelli, best known by his pseudonym Paul Kelly , the Eastman gang succeeded the long dominant Whyos as the first non- Irish street gang to gain prominence in the underworld during the 1890s.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang competed with the Five Points Gang for control of New York's underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: Monk Eastman operated a powerful Jewish gang known as the Eastman Gang that competed with Italian and Irish gangs , notably Paul Kelly 's Five Points Gang , for control of New York City's underworld.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang competed with the Five Points Gang for control of New York City's underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: Monk Eastman operated a powerful Jewish gang known as the Eastman Gang that competed with Italian and Irish gangs, notably Paul Kelly's Five Points Gang, for control of New York City's underworld.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang was at war with the Humpty Jackson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s

descriptions: Lenox Avenue Gang is listed as an ally of Eastman Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang , Lenox Avenue Gang

descriptions: Eastman Gang may have developed from the Short Tails.
edges.evidence_quote: Another gang of the era, the Short Tails , had its headquarters in this same area, and the Eastman gang may have developed from that group.

descriptions: Eastman Gang succeeded the Whyos as a prominent gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the Eastman gang succeeded the long dominant Whyos as the first non- Irish street gang to gain prominence in the underworld during the 1890s.

descriptions: Eastman Gang had rivalries with Yakey Yakes.
edges.evidence_quote: Eventually, the gang became involved in rivalries with other local gangs such as the Yakey Yakes and the Five Points Gang , warring over both territory and work as political sluggers for Tammany Hall.

descriptions: The two gangs fought over territory, engaging in open warfare.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivals disputed claims to a strip of territory of the Lower East Side of Manhattan.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang and Eastman Gang are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: This was in contrast to many of his fellow Jewish East Siders who predominantly joined the Five Points Gang's rival Eastman Gang led by its namesake Monk Eastman.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was frequently in conflict with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Ellos estaban frecuentemente en conflicto con la Eastman Gang de la misma área.

descriptions: The Lenox Avenue Gang was allied with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Eastman Gang

Abergil crime family.description: Israeli organized crime syndicate founded by brothers Yaakov, Avi, and Itzhak Abergil in Ramat Gan, Israel, in the 1990s. Involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and murder. Key leaders were arrested and extradited to the United States, with Itzhak Abergil receiving multiple life sentences in 2022.

descriptions: The Abdel-Kader family cooperates with Jewish crime families, including the Abergil crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Clans like Tayibe based Abdel-Kader family are involved in extortion, drug and weapon trafficking, fraud as well as money laundering, often in cooperation with their Jewish counterparts.

descriptions: Abergil organization is rival to Alperon crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Vineland Boys Rivals: Mexican Mafia, Alperon crime organization

descriptions: Alperon crime organization is a rival of the Abergil crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Alperon crime organization

descriptions: Jerusalem Gang member Gabi Ben Harosh ran the lending bank for the Abergil organization's money-laundering operation.
edges.evidence_quote: The money was given out as loans to Israeli businesspeople in the United States, who were later extorted to give up their businesses if they did not pay up. Meir Abergil, described as the person in charge of finances, collected the money that was exacted and later laundered and invested it. Gabi Ben Harosh of the "Jerusalem Gang" who was arrested in Los Angeles in 2004, ran the lending bank in the United States and updated Meir and Itzhak Abergil on its balance.

descriptions: Abergil organization is rival to Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Vineland Boys Rivals: Mexican Mafia, Alperon crime organization

descriptions: Mexican Mafia is a rival of the Abergil crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexican Mafia

descriptions: Abergil organization cooperated with Vineland Boys for drug supply and expansion.
edges.evidence_quote: US federal prosecutors claim that when the Abergils wished to expand their mob syndicate in the US, they turned for help to an upstart Latino street gang known as the Vineland Boys.

descriptions: Abergil crime family allied with Vineland Boyz street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Vineland Boys

descriptions: Both were involved in ecstasy trafficking in the US, but no direct cooperation or conflict is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Israeli crime organizations such as the Abergil crime family and Zeev Rosenstein were heavily involved in ecstasy trafficking in the United States.

descriptions: Vineland Boys formed an alliance with Abergil crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys have formed alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family.

descriptions: Vineland Boys teamed up with Abergil crime family in 2000 for protection and cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang allegedly first teamed up with Abergils in 2000, offering protection and cooperation to the Israeli mafia's ecstasy trafficking ring operating in their territory.

descriptions: The Madonia clan exchanged information with the Di Trapani clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Nevertheless, they have continued to run the Madonia clan, issuing orders via Di Trapani and exchanging information with the Di Trapani clan, according to investigators.

descriptions: Bronx '95 was given hangaround status by Bandidos as a precursor chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: Bronx '95 – a club founded in 1995 that was given hangaround status by the Bandidos and was intended to be the precursor to a Bandidos chapter in Trondheim

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Seaburn Casuals.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C. , the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals , as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters , a firm associated with Chelsea F.C. , and the Millwall Bushwackers , a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: Garfield Place Boys was one of the constituent gangs that formed the South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the gangs that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys were the South Brooklyn Devils, the Garfield Place Boys, the SB Angels, SB Diapers, the Wanderers, the Degraw St boys, the Sackett St Boys, the Butler Gents, the Gowanus Boys, the Kane St. Midgets, The Little Gents, and the Young Savages.

descriptions: Bonanno family took revenge on Buccellato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: After the murders of Stefano and Giuseppe Bonanno, their younger brother, Salvatore, took revenge by killing members of the Buccellatos.

descriptions: Magaddino clan feuded with Buccellato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1900s, the two clans feuded with Felice Buccellato, the boss of the Buccellato clan.

descriptions: The Castellammarese Mafia and the Buccellato clan were in violent conflict in Brooklyn.
edges.evidence_quote: Continued violence between the two groups caused Milazzo and Magaddino to flee New York City in 1921 after they were sought for questioning in connection with the murder of a Buccellato clan member.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, Bertrand Bisimwa emerged as one of the leaders of the new rebel group called M27, which is composed of members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) and M23 defectors.

Richardson Gang.description: English crime gang based in South London in the 1960s, founded by Charles Richardson. Known for extreme violence and torture methods such as pulling teeth and nailing victims to floors. Involved in racketeering, narcotics, extortion, and other crimes. Rivaled the Kray Firm.

descriptions: The Adams family ordered the shooting of a former enforcer of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The shooting of the then 68-year-old "Mad" Frankie Fraser, a former enforcer for The Richardson Gang, in July 1991 was said to have been ordered by the Adams family

descriptions: The two gangs were in a turf war, a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Richardson gang and the Kray twins were engaged in a turf war in the mid-to-late 1960s.

descriptions: Infobox lists Kray Firm as rivals of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kray Firm

descriptions: The Richardson Gang were direct rivals to the Kray Twins.
edges.evidence_quote: South London was controlled at that time by the Richardson Gang, who were seen as direct rivals to the Kray Twins.

descriptions: The Richardson Gang is described as a rival of the Krays' Firm.
edges.evidence_quote: At Shepton Mallet they met Charlie Richardson, another inmate, who later formed the Richardson Gang, a rival of the Krays’ Firm.

descriptions: Ronnie Kray killed a member of the Richardson Gang, indicating conflict between the two gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Ronnie shot and killed George Cornell, a member of the Richardson Gang, at the Blind Beggar pub in Whitechapel on 9 March 1966.

descriptions: Joey Pyle was associated with both groups, implying a cooperative relationship between them.
edges.evidence_quote: An associate of the Krays and the Richardsons

descriptions: Pyle mediated between the Krays and Richardsons, preventing conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Sometimes the Twins and the Richardsons would be at the same club at the same time but nothing ever sparked off while I was around.

descriptions: George Cornell switched from the Watney Streeters to ally with the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cornell was originally a member of an East End gang called "The Watney Streeters" and later became involved with the Krays. However, he changed sides around 1964 and allied himself with the Richardsons.

descriptions: Hezbollah and Amal are both major Shiite political parties in Lebanon.

Dead Man Incorporated.description: Dead Man Incorporated (DMI) is a predominantly white organized crime enterprise founded in the late 1990s in the Maryland Department of Corrections by Perry Roark, James Sweeney, and Brian Jordan. Initially allied with the Black Guerrilla Family, the group expanded into drug trafficking, murder, and contract killing. It operates in Maryland, Virginia, and West Virginia, with an estimated 370 members. Internal divisions have arisen over the gang's direction and ideology.

descriptions: Dead Man Incorporated is a rival of Aryan Brotherhood per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Guerrilla Family, Dead Man Incorporated, Black P. Stones, Bloods, Crips, D.C. Blacks, Nuestra Familia

descriptions: Dead Man Incorporated is listed as a current ally of BGF in the infobox.

descriptions: DMI was founded with permission from BGF, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Roark was a close associate of the Black Guerrilla Family and received permission from them to start an organization to unite white inmates in the system.

descriptions: DMI initially worked for BGF, showing cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Initially, DMI worked for the BGF, but soon began offering those services to other gangs by targeting rivals and correctional staff.

descriptions: Infobox lists BGF as an ally of DMI.

descriptions: Spangler Posse is a subset of Jamaican posses.
edges.evidence_quote: The two largest Jamaican posses in the U.S., the Shower Posse and the Spangler Posse, trace their origins to political parties in Kingston.

descriptions: The Shower Posse and Spangler Posse fought together against the Dog Posse and Tel Aviv Posse, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: elements of the Shower Posse and Spangler Posse from Brooklyn and the Bronx fought with the Boston-based Dog Posse and Tel Aviv Posse.

descriptions: Other posses are splinter groups of these two, indicating a structural relationship.
